---
number-depth: 2
format: html
---

## Contents

### Day 1

#### Morning {.unnumbered}

* **Introduction to the course (30 min):** This introductory session presents the course objectives, structure, and expected learning outcomes. 

* **Lecture: Introduction to Big Data Technologies for Active Mobility (90 minutes):** An introductory lecture will be given presenting the concept, characteristics and challenges of using big data. The lecture will also demonstrate the many applications in real-world practice and research of using big data in transportation including: i) sensors and video analytics; social-media; and iii) mobile phone and floating car data, among others. 

> **Materials:** The PowerPoints of these theorectical lectures will be shared to participants by email. 

* **Break (20 min)**

* **Interactive lecture \ workshop: Understanding Passive Mobility Big Data in Practice (60 min):** The session introduces participants to a real passive‑mobility dataset—such as Bluetooth sensor data tracking pedestrian movement—and guides them through the early stages of working with unfamiliar mobility big data. The focus is on understanding the dataset’s spatial and temporal structure, identifying general behavioural patterns, and critically evaluating its limitations and potential biases. Participants will discuss key data‑processing decisions, including what information to keep or filter depending on research or planning goals, and how to avoid losing valuable insights through overly aggressive data cleaning. They will also explore which research questions the dataset can answer and what complementary data sources might be needed for planning‑relevant interpretations.

> **Materials:** The PowerPoint of this theorectical/practical lecture will be shared to participants by email. 

#### Afternoon {.unnumbered}

* **Hands-on workshop in R (90 min):** The basics of R will be taught in this workshop, including how to import datasets, perform descriptive statistics and treatment (e.g., deal with missing data, outliers, etc). All the steps described in the previous session, will be reproduced and taught in this session using the R. The same dataset will be used in order for participants to understand and better relate the learnings from the previous session.

<!-- ### Day 2 -->

<!-- #### Morning {.unnumbered} -->

<!-- We will start the day by estimating the different types of [centroids of transport zones](centroids.qmd). -->

<!-- After this, the natural next step is to create [desire lines](desire-lines.qmd) from orgins and destinations of the transport zones. -->

<!-- We will then learn how to estimate [euclidean and routing distances](distances.qmd) for the desire-lines, using transport networks. -->

<!-- #### Afternoon {.unnumbered} -->

<!-- In the second afternoon, we will briefly learn where to find and extract [open transportation data](open-data.qmd), such as OpenStreetMap and GTFS. -->

<!-- Then, we will learn how to perform [accessibility analysis](r5r.qmd), using the r5r package. -->

<!-- And finally, to wrap up all this topics, we will have a group exercise using other complex datasets, where you will apply the knowledge learned during the course. -->

## Detailed schedule

| Day 1 |                                                                                                                 |
|---------|----------------------------------------------------------|
|  9.00 | Introductions and Presentation of the course contents                                                           |
|  9.30 | Introduction to Big Data Technologies for Active Mobility                                                      |                                 |
| 11.00 | *Coffee break*                                                                                                  |
| 11.20 | Interactive lecture: Understanding Passive Mobility Big Data in Practice |

| 12.30 | *Lunch break*                |
|       |                                                                                   
| 13.30 | Data manipulation: examples and exercises (select, filter, left-join, group and summarize, using dplyr package) |
| 15.30 | *Coffee break*                                                                                                  |
| 15.45 | Introduction to GIS and spatial data: import create vector data                                                 |
| 16.30 | View and export interactive maps                                                                                |
| 17.00 | *End of day 1*                                                                                                  |

| Day 2 |                                                                                    |
|----------|---------------------------------------------------------|
| 9.30  | Centroids of transport zones                                                       |
| 10.15 | Desire-lines from OD pairs and transport zones                                     |
| 11.00 | *Coffee break*                                                                     |
| 11.15 | (cont.)                                                                            |
| 11.30 | Euclidean and routing distances with sf and r5r                                    |
|       |                                                                                    |
| 12.30 | *Lunch break*                                                                      |
|       |                                                                                    |
| 13.30 | Open Transportation data: where to find it (OSM and GTFS)                          |
| 14.00 | Accessibility analysis with r5r                                                    |
| 16.00 | *Coffee break*                                                                     |
| 16.15 | Using you data: manipulation and spatial analysis methods and further applications |
| 16.45 | Survey and feedback from participants                                              |
| 17.00 | *End of day 2*                                                                     |

## Location

The course will take place at Campus Sterre, Building S8, room 2.4.

```{r}
#| fig-format: png
Campus_S8_coord = c(3.7105372, 51.0241258)
Campus_S8 = sf::st_sfc(sf::st_point(Campus_S8_coord)) # create point
Campus_S8 = sf::st_as_sf(Campus_S8, crs = 4326) # assign crs

mapview::mapview(Campus_S8, map.types = "OpenStreetMap") # quick map view
```

## Resources

-   You laptop, with any OS

-   Github repository with all the materials (data, code and guidelines)
